dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal requires re-entry after stopped-out position when market generates new directional signal (new high). Entry condition uses close > prev_high to detect new directional high on single bar. Exit uses 1% stop-loss via returns indicator. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT per original signal specification. The 'lookback_periods': 20 and 'confirmation_bars': 0 parameters are noted but confirmation_bars=0 aligns with 'any single bar' requirement. The new_high_threshold_pct of -0.25 appears to be a data artifact and is not applied as the core logic is directional breakout (new high), not a percentage threshold.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal requires re-entry after stopped-out position when market generates new directional signal (new high). Entry condition uses close > prev_high to detect new directional high on single bar. Exit uses 1% stop-loss via returns indicator. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT per original signal specification. The 'lookback_periods': 20 and 'confirmation_bars': 0 parameters are noted but confirmation_bars=0 aligns with 'any single bar' requirement. The new_high_threshold_pct of -0.25 appears to be a data artifact and is not applied as the core logic is directional breakout (new high), not a percentage threshold.

source_rule_text: A trader should re-enter a stopped-out position only after the market generates a new directional signal (such as a new high) rather than attempting to re-enter on any reversal, to avoid whipsaw and ensure alignment with the system's intent.
